name: performance-optimization
description: Measure-first performance optimization with profiling workflows

positive_triggers.input: Users are reporting the page is slow, help investigate
Lighthouse score dropped from 90 to 65 in this release
P95 latency on the API has gone up 3x in production

negative_triggers.input: I think this loop could be faster
One user reported the page was slow once
negative_triggers.reason: Single anecdotal report, not a performance issue yet

expected_behavior: Agent defines target metric (LCP / FID / CLS / TTI / P95) and threshold
Agent measures baseline (Lighthouse / WebPageTest / k6), records 3+ runs
Agent profiles (DevTools / py-spy / pprof) to find ACTUAL bottleneck
Agent does NOT optimize without measurement
Agent applies one change at a time, measures after each
Agent confirms improvement is statistically significant
Agent adds guard (perf budget in CI / monitoring)

behavioral_evals.scenario: User reports: 'P95 latency up 3x in production'
expected_behavior: Agent checks observability for context (deployments / traffic changes / deps)
Agent profiles production (anonymized) or staging reproduction
Agent identifies specific bottleneck (DB query / N+1 / GC / external API)
Agent applies targeted fix, not blanket optimization
Agent monitors P95 after deploy to confirm fix

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
